name: Seeing they see not
notes: Isaiah's commission (Isa 6:9-10) is among the most-cited Old Testament passages in the New Testament, applied wherever men hear the word yet do not perceive. Isaiah 6:9 supplies the paired refrain of hearing without understanding and seeing without perceiving, echoed in Matt 13:14, Mark 4:12, Luke 8:10, and Acts 28:26. Isaiah 6:10 supplies the heart waxed gross, the eyes closed, and the purpose 'lest they should see ... and be converted, and be healed,' echoed in Matt 13:15, John 12:40, and Acts 28:27. The citations stand in complementary form: Matthew and Acts quote Isaiah at fullest length, Mark and Luke compress the refrain, and John reads it as God's act ('He hath blinded their eyes, and hardened their heart'), each a true vantage on the same prophecy. The differing forms in which the Spirit recorded the citation are complementary and left as a matter for further study. Labels carry the exact wording shared by each linked pair.

links.label: ye shall ... and shall not understand ... ye shall see ... and shall not perceive
